title: Pyrus khumbuensis
intro: Pyrus khumbuensis is a species of wild apple tree native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its unique combination of morphological and molecular features. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus khumbuensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pyrus khumbuensis is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to relieve respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pyrus khumbuensis is a species of pear tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is classified within the genus Pyrus, which comprises various species of pear trees. The classification of Pyrus khumbuensis places it in the order Rosales, along with other related plants such as roses and apples. Further classification also groups it under the subfamily Amygdaloideae.
